mechanism_narrative: A rising federal funds rate over the preceding 3 months increases the risk-free discount rate, reducing the present value of future corporate cash flows and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains above -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following rising FEDFUNDS are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises by more than 0.3 percentage points from its 12-month low, labor market deterioration signals weakening aggregate income and consumption, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If triggered instances produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50% of occurrences over any 3-year window, or if the signal's Rank IC is not statistically negative (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month declines in real retail sales and industrial production indicate a broad demand contraction that reduces corporate revenue expectations, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and INDPRO both decline month-over-month
failure_condition: If the joint signal of RSAFS and INDPRO both declining does not produce negative 20-day returns in at least 55% of instances, or if the interaction term's t-statistic is not significant (p > 0.05) in a 2-year rolling regression.

mechanism_narrative: Elevated inflation measured by CPIAUCSL predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days only when the federal funds rate is above 4.5%, because the Federal Reserve is constrained from easing by its inflation mandate.
failure_condition: If month-over-month CPIAUCSL growth fails to predict negative 5-day returns in regimes where FEDFUNDS > 4.5% (Rank IC < -0.03), or if the signal performs equally well when FEDFUNDS <= 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production over the preceding 3 months indicates expanding manufacturing capacity utilization and corporate earnings potential, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If 3-month INDPRO momentum is not positively correlated with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in nonfarm payrolls signals expanding household income and aggregate demand, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: If month-over-month PAYEMS changes are not positively correlated with 5-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.03) over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is elevated above 5.0%, simultaneous declines in housing starts and retail sales indicate that tight monetary policy is choking off demand in interest-sensitive sectors, amplifying negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If the triple interaction does not produce negative 20-day returns in more than 55% of triggered instances, or if the marginal effect of HOUST and RSAFS jointly is not significantly negative when FEDFUNDS > 5.0% (interaction p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month drop in housing starts below 1,400 thousand units signals a severe contraction in residential investment and downstream demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If HOUST readings below 1,400 do not coincide with negative 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of historical instances over a 5-year window.
